text.title: mlp-planning Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for mlp-planning as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Adding more features to an MVP instead of polishing existing ones to MLP threshold.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Feature addition expands surface area for friction without addressing the root retention problem. Most MVPs reach MLP by improving the core 3 features to Layer 4, not by adding a 4th feature.

failure-mode.description: Surface-level polish (new colors, marketing copy) while ignoring interaction quality (latency, defaults, error recovery).
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Visual polish is visible but does not compound; interaction quality (speed, anticipation, recovery) affects every session and compounds with usage frequency.

failure-mode.description: Skipping numeric MLP completion criteria before starting delight work.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without exit criteria, "MLP" expands indefinitely — teams keep polishing without shipping. The criteria are the commitment mechanism that ends the sprint.

failure-mode.description: Generic delight (confetti everywhere) not tailored to the user persona.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: B2B enterprise users find confetti unprofessional; Japanese users find animated celebrations intrusive. Delight must be calibrated to the specific persona's emotional context.
